prose:
Method provenance
Property: Atom-condensed condensed Fukui function indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) for formaldehyde; headline value is f⁺ of the most electrophilic atom (C, atom 2).
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier “standard”), density-fitted RIJ.
Geometry provenance: fixed geometry read from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz (no re-optimization; single-point population analyses for N, N+1, and N–1 electron counts performed on that structure).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet for N-electron state (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation and anion states run at charge +1/−1, multiplicity 2 (doublets).
Software/backend: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF 2.x backend for DFT and Mulliken charges).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; integrity.status = "ok" and integrity.trustworthy = true.  Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.  The sums of f⁺ and f⁻ equal 1.0000, indicating charge conservation; the warning merely notes the expected value.  Mulliken charges are method- and basis-dependent; absolute magnitudes are qualitative.

Headline result
• Most electrophilic site: Carbon (atom 2) with f⁺ = 0.7083913 (dimensionless).
• Most nucleophilic site: Oxygen (atom 1) with f⁻ = 0.46742464 (dimensionless).

Reproducibility
cli invocation: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpqpnmiy9k/live.json
JSON result: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpqpnmiy9k/live.json
